message:
fix(ci): allow release job to proceed when non-critical builds fail

The release job had `needs: [plan, build]` without an `if:` guard,
causing it to be skipped whenever any matrix entry (e.g., Windows)
failed — even when all primary targets (Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64,
macOS) built successfully. Add `if: always()` so the GitHub Release
is created with whatever artifacts are available.

Refs #34

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -290,6 +290,7 @@ jobs:
   release:
     name: Create GitHub Release
     needs: [plan, build]
+    if: always() && needs.plan.result == 'success' && !cancelled()
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 10
     steps:
